nodes.context: Camorra clan based in Casal di Principe and San Cipriano d'Aversa, founded by Antonio Bardellino.
Italian organized crime syndicate based in Campania.
Confederation of Camorra clans formed to oppose the Nuova Camorra Organizzata.
Camorra organization led by Raffaele Cutolo.
Camorra clan from Marano, allied with the Corleonesi.
Camorra clan from Saviano led by Carmine Alfieri.
Camorra clan from Poggiomarino led by Pasquale Galasso.
Camorra clan from Naples' Forcella quarter led by Luigi Giuliano.
Camorra clan from Portici led by Luigi Vollaro.
American Mafia family based in New York.
Sicilian Mafia faction from Corleone led by Luciano Leggio and Salvatore Riina.

edges.evidence_quote: Antonio Bardellino ... was a powerful Camorrista and boss of the Casalesi clan
edges.context: The Casalesi clan is a Camorra clan.

edges.evidence_quote: He was one of the few Camorra bosses who were also initiated into Cosa Nostra.
edges.context: Bardellino, boss of Casalesi, was initiated into Cosa Nostra, indicating a structural tie.

edges.evidence_quote: Bardellino was the main exponent of the Nuova Famiglia (NF), a confederation of clans that was formed to contrast the growing power of the predominant Nuova Camorra Organizzata (NCO), led by Raffaele Cutolo.
edges.context: NF was formed to oppose NCO, resulting in a war.

edges.evidence_quote: Bardellino was the main exponent of the Nuova Famiglia (NF)
edges.context: Bardellino, boss of Casalesi, was the main exponent of NF, so Casalesi is part of NF.

edges.evidence_quote: the NF alliance soon disintegrated, with a war breaking out between the Bardellino and Nuvoletta clans towards the end of 1983.
edges.context: War between Casalesi (Bardellino) and Nuvoletta clan.

edges.evidence_quote: Heroin was smuggled as well, and shipments to the Gambino crime family were concealed inside espresso filters.
edges.context: Casalesi clan smuggled heroin to the Gambino crime family.

edges.evidence_quote: the Nuvoletta brothers, who were allied with the Corleonesi headed by Luciano Leggio and Salvatore Riina
edges.context: Nuvoletta clan was allied with the Corleonesi.

edges.evidence_quote: Bardellino was allied with Rosario Riccobono, Stefano Bontade, Gaetano Badalamenti, and Tommaso Buscetta, all heads of fallen Palermo families which were defeated by the Corleonesi
edges.context: Casalesi clan (Bardellino) was opposed to the Corleonesi, as he was allied with their enemies.

edges.evidence_quote: the NF alliance soon disintegrated, with a war breaking out between the Bardellino and Nuvoletta clans
edges.context: NF included Nuvoletta clan initially, but later war broke out between Bardellino (NF leader) and Nuvoletta.

edges.evidence_quote: The NF consisted of Bardellino, Michele Zaza ... the Gionta clan
edges.context: Gionta clan was a member of the Nuova Famiglia confederation.

edges.evidence_quote: The NF consisted of Bardellino, Michele Zaza ... the Alfieri clan
edges.context: Alfieri clan was a member of the Nuova Famiglia confederation.

edges.evidence_quote: The NF consisted of Bardellino, Michele Zaza ... the Galasso clan
edges.context: Galasso clan was a member of the Nuova Famiglia confederation.

edges.evidence_quote: The NF consisted of Bardellino, Michele Zaza ... the Giuliano clan
edges.context: Giuliano clan was a member of the Nuova Famiglia confederation.

edges.evidence_quote: The NF consisted of Bardellino, Michele Zaza ... the Vollaro clan
edges.context: Vollaro clan was a member of the Nuova Famiglia confederation.

edges.evidence_quote: an attack at Torre Annunziata ... led to the massacre of eight members of the Gionta clan allied with the Nuvolettas
edges.context: Gionta clan was allied with Nuvoletta clan.
